name: Ford, Clarence
clubs: President of W. S. S. Organization

name: Heath, Eunice
clubs: Secretary of Garland Society, first term
Cabinet member of Y. W. C. A.

name: Goodwin, Ruth
clubs: critic of Yuma Society first term, 1917

name: Hale, Alfred
clubs: president of Garland Society fourth term 1917
president of Garland Society fourth term, 1918

name: Marvin, Hamilton
clubs: President of Yuma Society first term, 1917
Vice-president of Y. M. C. A. 1917-18
Assistant business manager of Monitor Staff 1918
Member of Y. M. C. A. cabinet 1918

name: Sanders, Freddie
description: Freddie is a loyal Yuma and an energetic student. She numbers her friends by the scores.

name: Hodge, Mattie
description: A more energetic, positive and dependable student was never enrolled. Devotion to duty characterizes her everyday life. Being found always in her place, nobody learned to doubt her. She takes active part in all student organizations. Her departure means that a missing link will occur in our student life next year.

name: Shell, Mary
clubs: President of Y. W. C. A. 1917
Secretary of Garland Society third term, 1917

name: Green, Lottie
description: Lottie has been with us three years, during which time she has won her way into the hearts and lives of us all. She fully measures up to what we regard as a noble young lady. We would be glad to keep her with us longer, but such pleasures are ruthlessly denied us by that inevitable happening—graduation.

name: Camp, Cecil
description: Rarely does any school number among its students a more loyal, ambitious, earnest and worthy one than Cecil. In literary society, in classroom, and on the athletic field, wherever found, she is ever bubbling over with school spirit and wholesome fun.

name: Long, Carrie
description: During her sojourn with us, Carrie has built up many friendships of that enduring kind which we all covet. We regret to lose her another year, but our loss is the gain of others with whom she will be associated henceforth. Being sincere, kind and true, her connection with us has been most kind and satisfactory.

name: Arris, Owen
description: He has a sweet and tender voice And a dear loving smile; He takes special interest In a little "Fairchild."

name: Nicie, Davis
description: Of all the things she'd rather be She'd rather be a Sailor; She thinks she'd get to go abroad With one boy—Oscar Taylor.

name: Odessa, Pearce
description: When you see her smile widely And look sweeter than cakes, You may know she has news From her man at Great Lakes.

name: Ye, Adams
description: She's some class editor, You bet it! The Juniors of '18 she attempted to edit, She sighed and she moaned And she shed some big tears, When she thought of the futures Of their awful careers.

name: Lewis, Grace
motto: "She's hunting a husband, One of great fame; A 'regular heart smasher' Is her middle name."

name: Taylor, Doswell
motto: "He's attentive and studious, But no end of a flirt; Just call him a 'slacker,' And his feelings you'll hurt."

name: Hurst, Margaret
motto: "Her long-suit is eating Mashed potatoes and gravy, But while she devours it Her heart's in the navy."

name: Pearce, Garland
motto: "When he gets all dolled up And looks at his best, I'm sure you will say, (When it comes to the test) That he's the best looking Of all boys in the land, And to cap it all off, a real 'ladies' man.'"

name: Wallace, Mary
description: She is very nice looking And not at all bad, But she can't keep her seat When she hears the name "Frad."

name: Yocum, Donald
description: You may know he's quite young By his soft, tender face, When the rules are suspended He's always in place.

name: Simms, Mary
description: You'll notice she's not very small in size, She works every day to try to get wise; She's indeed very lucky in securing a beau, And her ideal husband is one Mr. Lowe.

name: Beene, Julia
description: She is indeed very handsome and tall, When a good time is coming She answers the call; If she's treated unfair She has nothing to say, But she goes to her parlor And a rag she doth play.
